summary:
The dimensionally most stable elastomer is addition silicone, which maintains integrity well even after storage, while polsulfide exhibits severe shrinkage requiring immediate use. The correct descending order based on available data is addition silicone > polyether > condensation silicone > polysulfide.  

(Note: No provided option exactly reflects this sequence.)
